zh.lifeSummary: Adam De Hartley於大約1188年出生在英國多瑟特明特尼 馬尼亞。他於1217年與Cecilia在英國英格蘭多瑟特明特尼 馬尼亞結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世在英國戴文哈特雷。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hartley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name, in northern England mainly from Hartley in Rochdale parish (Lancashire) but also from any of the places called Hartley in Westmorland and the West Yorkshire. In southern England it derives Hartley in Devon, Hampshire, and Kent, and from Hartleigh in Devon. Similar placenames occur in Berkshire, Dorset, and Northumberland, but it is not known if they gave rise to surnames. Most of the placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, though the Westmorland placename comes from Old English heard ‘hard’ + clā ‘claw, tongue of land’, and the Northumberland placename derives from Old English heorot + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArtghaile ‘descendant of Artghal’, a personal name composed of the elements Art ‘bear’ or ‘hero’ + gal ‘valor’.

variants.name: Artley
meanings: English: variant of Hartley with loss of initial H-.

variants.name: Hartless
meanings: English (Staffordshire): variant of Hartley or of Hartle , with post-medieval excrescent -s. The spelling of the modern surname is no doubt due to confusion with the English vocabulary word heartless, meaning ‘cruel or unfeeling’.

nameMeanings.name: Adam
meanings: Hebrew: The Scripture name, in Hebrew, Adham, signifying 'man;' literally earthy, red earth; Rudolph, however, says the name in Ethiopic means ' to be fair, beautiful.' Hence the nurse-name Addy, little Adam.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Adam De Hartley was born about 1188, in Minterne Magna, Dorset, England. He married Cecilia in 1217, in Minterne Magna, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 6 daughters. He died in Hartley, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Adam De Hartleyは約1188年にイギリスドールセトミンテルン マグナで生まれた。1217年にイギリスイギリスドールセトミンテルン マグナでCeciliaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスデボンハートリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Adam De Hartley(은)는 대략 1188년 영국 도르세트 민터니 매그나에서 태어났다. 그는 1217년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 민터니 매그나에서 Cecilia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 데폰 하틀리에서 사망했다.
